reasoning: The market estimated 1% on Oct 30, right before the Nov 9 cutoff. Copernicus just released their October 2025 anomaly update on Nov 6, showing that October 2025 was the 3rd warmest on record, 0.16 C cooler than October 2023, and 0.11 C cooler than October 2024. For this market to resolve YES, the GISTEMP anomaly (1951-1980 baseline usually) must fall exactly between 1.10 and 1.14 C. Narrow temperature brackets on Polymarket for monthly readings have historically traded around 1% when they are far from the expected value or once the leading indicators (like Copernicus/ERA5) already strongly suggest a different bucket. Given the market's heavy discount and the tight 0.05 C window, the probability is extremely low. I will output 0.02 to respect bounds and reflect the near impossibility.
